scenario_title: Within-message ordering: add then delete same RR versus delete then add
test_rationale: The zone starts with a single TXT rrset at ord.campus.edu. so the UPDATE can target an existing owner without needing additional setup. The UPDATE adds a new TXT RR and then deletes that same RR in the same message, which is exactly the unspecified ordering/canonicalization case. A server that applies changes strictly in order will leave the preexisting rrset unchanged and answer NOERROR, while a server that treats the conflict declaratively may reject the message or otherwise diverge. The follow-up QUERY makes the final rrset observable.

group_1.behavior: Processes the UPDATE in message order; the add is applied and then the delete removes the same RR, leaving only the original TXT "base" rrset.

group_2.behavior: Canonicalizes or rejects the conflicting intra-message add/delete pair instead of honoring strict message order.
group_2.post_state: ord.campus.edu. TXT rrset is not changed from the initial "base" state.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 describes UPDATE as a sequence of additions and deletions, but it does not define how a server must resolve conflicting operations on the same RR within one message. RFC 2136 §7.2 discusses prerequisites as the mechanism for expressing dependencies, yet it does not mandate an intra-Update Section ordering rule. As a result, servers may apply operations sequentially, collapse them semantically, or reject the message, yielding divergent reply codes and/or final zone state.
